mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rates above 0.5% monthly consistently coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns during expansion regimes, invalidating the profit transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust employment expansion, supporting wage growth and consumer demand, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across all market conditions over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policy, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5%, demonstrating market tolerance for elevated inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal spending reveals underlying demand resilience; when RSAFS outpaces GDPC1 trends, consumer-driven earnings revisions push S&P 500 returns positive over 20 days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-GDPC1 divergence signal produces negative forward returns in 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints and reduced corporate output, triggering immediate sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles consistently precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.
